title: Packet 027: Builder restart/recovery proof
description: Prove that a Builder run that crashes mid-attempt can be restarted without losing evidence or consuming extra budget. The operator sees exactly where the crash happened and whether recovery succeeded. This packet closes the restart/recovery gap left by Packet 026 (reliability rails).

packets.title: Stale running attempt reconciliation on entry
packets.objective: When run_packet or a new run is opened, detect any stale 'running' attempts left by crashed workers and transition them to 'failed' with infrastructure_failed event before opening a new attempt. The crashed attempt's run-manifest.json must be preserved with outcome:'crashed' and the crash reason. The attempt budget must not be consumed by infrastructure crashes (counts_toward_budget: False).
acceptance_criteria: run_packet detects stale running attempts on entry and marks them failed with infrastructure_failed event
A crashed attempt's run-manifest.json is preserved with outcome: crashed and the crash reason
initiative_status correctly reports the crashed attempt's evidence (not silently dropping it)
The attempt budget is not consumed by infrastructure crashes (counts_toward_budget: False)
python3.12 -m pytest tests/test_builder_loop.py tests/test_builder_runner.py -q passes
packets.forbidden_paths_note: Do not modify .claude/STATE.md, .claude/HANDOFF.md, or any runner-owned state files. These are managed by the orchestrator lifecycle.

policy.attempt_3_authorization: Operator-authorized retry: attempt 1 was interrupted (EXIT trap deleted result), attempt 2 failed due to manifest scope-definition error (test_builder_attempt.py not in allowed paths). Attempt 3 corrects the manifest and re-runs.

packets.title: Real restart/recovery exercise
packets.objective: Execute a real crash-recovery cycle: start a Builder attempt, kill the worker process mid-run, re-enter run_packet, verify the crashed attempt is marked failed with evidence preserved, a new attempt opens, and the budget is unchanged. This is the integration proof that the reconciliation logic works end-to-end.
acceptance_criteria: A test starts an attempt, simulates a crash (SIGTERM/killed worker), and re-enters run_packet
After recovery: crashed attempt is marked failed with infrastructure_failed event
After recovery: new attempt opens successfully
After recovery: attempt budget is unchanged (crash did not consume budget)
After recovery: crash evidence is preserved in run-manifest.json with outcome: crashed
python3.12 -m pytest tests/test_builder_loop.py -q passes

packets.title: Bounded infrastructure-recovery budget
packets.objective: Implement a separate bounded infrastructure-recovery budget that limits how many consecutive infrastructure crashes are tolerated before the run stops with a truthful blocker. Repeated identical crashes must stop with a clear error message, not loop forever. This prevents infinite crash-recovery loops.
acceptance_criteria: Infrastructure crashes are tracked separately from implementation attempt budget
After N consecutive identical crashes (configurable, default 3), the run stops with a truthful blocker
The blocker message includes the crash count, the crash reason, and the fact that recovery was attempted
Non-identical crashes (different error messages) are tracked independently
python3.12 -m pytest tests/test_builder_loop.py tests/test_builder_runner.py -q passes

packets.title: No stale implementation/review artifact reuse
packets.objective: Ensure that after a crash and recovery, the new attempt does not reuse stale implementation artifacts (code changes, review results, or evidence) from the crashed attempt. Each attempt must start clean. The review binding (from Packet 026/0173) must reject reviews of stale SHAs.
acceptance_criteria: A new attempt after crash recovery starts with a clean worktree (no stale code changes)
Implementation artifacts from the crashed attempt are not carried into the new attempt
Review binding rejects reviews of stale SHAs after recovery
python3.12 -m pytest tests/test_builder_loop.py tests/test_builder_context.py -q passes

packets.title: Truthful task and initiative closeout
packets.objective: Ensure that after a successful crash-recovery cycle, the task and initiative can be truthfully closed out: the final state reflects the actual outcome (success after recovery, or blocked after exhausting recovery budget). No stale evidence is carried forward. The initiative status rollup correctly reports the final state.
acceptance_criteria: After successful recovery and implementation, the task closes with outcome: succeeded
After exhausting recovery budget, the task closes with outcome: blocked and a truthful reason
initiative_status rollup correctly reports the final state (not stuck in running)
No stale evidence from crashed attempts appears in the closeout
python3.12 -m pytest tests/test_builder_initiative.py tests/test_builder_loop.py -q passes
